column6: Cairo status accessors return declared cairo_status_t values; the typed public API cannot inject an unknown integer.
Cairo status accessors return declared cairo_status_t values; the typed public API cannot inject an unknown integer.
The portable release matrix has no DRM Cairo device constructor or display server capable of producing this backend.
The portable release matrix has no XCB Cairo device constructor or display server capable of producing this backend.
Cairo owns device type values and public wrappers cannot construct a device carrying an unknown native type integer.
Cairo script devices return only ASCII or binary mode; public raw setters are checked before this decoder runs.
Cairo status accessors return declared cairo_status_t values; the typed public API cannot inject an unknown integer.
Cairo toy font faces return only declared slant values and no public API can mutate the native enum storage.
Cairo toy font faces return only declared weight values and no public API can mutate the native enum storage.
Cairo status accessors return declared cairo_status_t values; the typed public API cannot inject an unknown integer.
Cairo before 1.16 rejects the variations getter before it can return the no-variation branch introduced by newer Cairo.
Cairo before 1.16 rejects the variations getter before it can return copied variation text from newer Cairo.
Cairo before 1.18 rejects color-mode getters in the native compatibility stub before the typed decoder is entered.
Cairo before 1.18 rejects color-mode getters before the decoder can defend against an unknown native color-mode value.
Cairo before 1.17.2 cannot construct the floating-point RGB image format, so no public getter can return raw format 6.
Cairo validates raw image formats at construction and never stores an unknown value for the typed getter to decode.
Cairo status accessors return declared cairo_status_t values; the typed public API cannot inject an unknown integer.
Paths are copied from Cairo-owned path data whose element tags are constrained to the declared cairo_path_data_type_t values.
Cairo status accessors return declared cairo_status_t values; the typed public API cannot inject an unknown integer.
Cairo before 1.18 raises from the raw dither getter before the public typed conversion call can complete.
Cairo before 1.18 rejects dither getters in the native compatibility stub before the typed decoder is entered.
Cairo before 1.18 rejects dither getters before the decoder can defend against an unknown native dither value.
Cairo 1.15.10 reports only PDF 1.4 and 1.5, so its supported-version query cannot yield the later raw value 2.
The native supported-version query returns only declared PDF version values and rejects unknown raw ids before decoding.
The native supported-level query returns only declared PostScript levels and rejects unknown raw ids before decoding.
Cairo status accessors return declared cairo_status_t values; the typed public API cannot inject an unknown integer.
Cairo region containment returns exactly In, Out, or Part; the fallback protects against an ABI violation that public inputs cannot induce.
Cairo status accessors return declared cairo_status_t values; the typed public API cannot inject an unknown integer.
The portable toy and FreeType font paths emit forward clusters; this decoder preserves backward output from backends that report flag one.
Cairo text-to-glyphs returns only no-flags or backward; public callers cannot inject the native result flag.
The C glue creates exactly two position doubles per glyph; only corrupted native result storage can violate this length invariant.
The C glue creates exactly two integers per cluster; only corrupted native result storage can violate this length invariant.
The cleanup error arm is reachable only if a native result violates a guarded decode invariant after Cairo has reported success.
All pointer-bearing public calls require live external objects or validated buffers, so they cannot pass a null Cairo pointer.
Public Path values are produced by validated Cairo copies and cannot carry malformed native path records in the Cairo 1.18 release profile.
Raw content constructors are validated in their dedicated C glue and do not return this value through the shared status decoder.
InvalidVisual is emitted by display-system backends that are outside the portable image and vector release matrix.
TempFileError requires an operating-system temporary-file fault that deterministic portable tests do not inject.
Typed font face and scaled font wrappers preserve their Cairo subtype and cannot form a mismatched native font pair.
Cairoon does not expose a user-font callback constructor that could deliberately leave a required backend callback absent.
Cairo before 1.16 predates the PNG-specific status value, so no native operation in that profile can return raw status 39.
Cairoon exposes no external FreeType-face constructor, so public inputs cannot deliberately induce this backend-specific failure.
DwriteError is defined in the Cairo 1.18 status range but cannot occur on the Linux and macOS release lanes.
Cairo status accessors return declared cairo_status_t values; the typed public API cannot inject an unknown integer.
Xlib surfaces require a live X display and are not constructible in the portable headless release matrix.
The OS/2 Cairo backend is unavailable on supported Linux and macOS release platforms.
The Qt Cairo backend is unavailable in the linked portable Cairo builds used by the release matrix.
The OpenVG Cairo backend is unavailable in the linked portable Cairo builds used by the release matrix.
The Cairo XML backend is not enabled in the linked portable Cairo builds used by the release matrix.
Cairo owns surface type values and public wrappers cannot construct a surface carrying an unknown native type integer.
Cairo validates raw content values at construction and never stores an unknown value for the typed getter to decode.
The native supported-version query returns only declared SVG versions and rejects unknown raw ids before decoding.
Cairo ignores invalid raw SVG unit setters and therefore never returns an unknown unit to the typed decoder.
